title: lithocarpus cerifer
intro: Lithocarpus cerifer, also known as the Nutmeg or Bur Oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Taiwan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Lithocarpus cerifer has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Lithocarpus cerifer typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, or using the bark and leaves to create a decoction for medicinal purposes.
